questions.question: Which set of regulations requires your employer to make a suitable and sufficient assessment of working alone, reviewed when the person you support, the property or the round changes?
options: CQC Regulation 18
The Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999
RIDDOR 2013
The Corporate Manslaughter and Corporate Homicide Act 2007

questions.question: Where a gross breach of a duty of care causes a death, and senior management failings are a substantial part of it, under which law can the organisation be prosecuted?
options: Section 8 of the Health and Safety at Work etc. Act 1974
CQC Regulation 20
The Corporate Manslaughter and Corporate Homicide Act 2007
RIDDOR 2013

questions.question: Under regulation 10 of the Management of Health and Safety at Work Regulations 1999, what does your employer owe you about lone-working risks?
options: Clear information on the risks and the controls in place
Nothing, provided the assessment is kept on file in the office
A guarantee that you will never be asked to work alone
A signed disclaimer in which you accept the risks of working alone

questions.question: According to the course, what is de-escalation ultimately about?
options: Winning the argument by staying calmer than the other person
Physically holding the person until they calm down
Buying time so that you can leave safely
Persuading the person to apologise for their behaviour

questions.question: You break down on a fast road on your way to a call. What does the guidance advise?
options: Leave the vehicle by the nearside door and wait behind a barrier
Stand in front of the vehicle so that oncoming traffic can see you
Cross to the central reservation and wait there
Stay in the driver's seat with the hazard lights on until help arrives

questions.question: How does HSE guidance on lone working (INDG73) treat the time you spend getting to and from a call?
options: As part of the job, which your employer should support you to do safely
As the worker's own private time, outside health and safety
As unpaid time over which the employer has no duty
As the responsibility of the service user you are visiting

questions.question: Why does clutter and hoarding in a service user's home matter to a lone worker?
options: It automatically means the visit must be carried out by two workers
Narrow walkways and blocked exits make moving and handling unsafe and slow any evacuation
It is the worker's job to tidy and clear the walkways every visit
It is a sign the person no longer has the capacity to consent to care

questions.question: You smell gas inside a service user's home. What does the material tell you to do?
options: Finish the visit quickly, then mention it at the end of your round
Leave, ring the national gas emergency line and tell the office
Switch the lights on to check the cooker and boiler
Open the windows and carry on with the essential care

questions.question: A dog bites you on the way into a call. The wound looks minor and you feel fine. What does the guidance say?
options: Only report it if it later becomes infected
Wait until the end of the round before deciding what to do
Clean it yourself and carry on, as it is too small to matter
Get medical attention anyway, because bites can look minor at the time

questions.question: Reporting a near miss matters just as much as reporting an actual incident, because near misses are what shape the next risk assessment.
options: True
False

questions.question: If a risk assessment says a visit needs two people, a single worker may still attempt it alone as long as they feel confident.
options: True
False

questions.question: Which of the following are personal-safety habits the material recommends when walking or using public transport between calls? (Select all that apply)
options: Wear headphones so that you appear relaxed and unbothered
Keep your head up and hands free, with headphones out, especially in the dark
Take a quiet alley shortcut to reach the next visit faster
On the bus, sit near the driver or other passengers
Tuck your ID lanyard away between calls

questions.question: When writing up a lone-working incident, what does the material say you should do? (Select all that apply)
options: Complete the organisation's accident and incident form, the same day if you can
Stick to facts and direct quotes rather than opinions about why someone behaved as they did
Record the date, time and place, and exactly what happened and in what order
Wait until your next shift so that you can describe it more calmly
Send a brief text to your manager instead of completing a form

questions.question: After an incident, make yourself ___ first: leave the property and put distance between you and the risk before you do anything else.
The course sums up the response when you feel unsafe in three steps: leave, ___, report.
